records.display_name: Three LIRR branches now serve Elmont-UBS

payload.claim_text: Islander fans and concert-goers now have the option of three LIRR branches - Hempstead, Port Jefferson/Huntington and Ronkonkoma - to take them directly to and from Elmont-UBS Station
payload.subject: Elmont-UBS Station service

records.display_name: Over $5 billion in capital plan for ADA upgrades

payload.claim_text: More than $5 billion in the current Capital Plan is earmarked for Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessibility upgrade projects at stations, with $500 million of that dedicated for the commuter railroads.
payload.subject: MTA Capital Plan

records.display_name: Park Avenue Viaduct carries 98% of Metro-North service

payload.claim_text: The Park Avenue Viaduct carries all of Metro-North's Grand Central Terminal trains, which comprise 98% of the service the railroad operates, but is a single point of failure.
payload.subject: Park Avenue Viaduct

records.display_name: Bellerose Ladder enables Elmont-UBS full-time service

payload.claim_text: Once LIRR service to GCM begins at the end of the year, these new signals will make it possible for us to turn Elmont-UBS into a full-time station with service on the Hempstead Branch 365 days a year.
payload.subject: Elmont-UBS Station

9.8 miles of new third track on the LIRR Main Line between Carle Place and Hicksville. Completed on October 3, 2022. The project opened on time and $100 million under budget. Enables a 41% systemwide service increase when Grand Central Madison opens.
status: phased opening in progress, third section opening fall 2022

payload.description: Third and final section of LIRR's Main Line Third Track between Carle Place and Hicksville opened on October 3, 2022. Project opened on time and $100 million under budget.

records.raw_text: The third and final section of LIRR's Main Line Third Track between Carle Place and Hicksville opened on Monday, October 3, with Governor Hochul presiding at a ribbon-cutting ceremony in Westbury. This project opened on time and $100 million under budget, unlocking huge benefits for LIRR customers and operations, especially when taken together with the opening of Grand Central Madison service before the end of this year.

records.display_name: Transit Tech Lab

description: A public-private initiative created by the MTA and Partnership for New York City to bring private sector innovation to improve public transit. Two smartphone app pilots through Transit Tech Lab Accessibility Challenge: NaviLens wayfinding app tested on M23 SBS bus line with QR-style codes for blind/low vision customers, and Evelity indoor bluetooth beacon-based wayfinding app at Jay St-MetroTech Station for step-by-step station navigation.

records.raw_text: The two smartphone apps we are piloting through the Transit Tech Lab Accessibility Challenge are almost ready for customer testing. The NaviLens wayfinding app is being tested on the M23 SBS bus line; using colorful QR-style codes, NaviLens helps customers who are blind or low vision navigate to the exact locations of bus stops. We are additionally testing Evelity, an indoor bluetooth beacon-based wayfinding app at Jay St-MetroTech Station.

records.display_name: Park Avenue Viaduct Repair

description: The Park Avenue Viaduct carries all of Metro-North's Grand Central Terminal trains, which comprise 98% of the service the railroad operates. This state-of-good-repair effort will ensure the safety of the public and the railroad, improve commuter rail reliability, build more resilient infrastructure, and reduce noise and vibration levels by using modern materials. Major construction expected to begin in fall of 2023, repaired viaduct anticipated to be in service in 2027.

Replace the elevated steel structure that carries four tracks along Park Avenue between East 110 Street and the Harlem River Lift Bridge. Major construction expected to begin fall 2023, anticipated in service 2027.

NYC legal agreements executed in July 2022. Inspection and flag reports completed and submitted. Design-Build Construction Contract awarded in December 2022. The Park Avenue Viaduct is the main artery of the Metro-North system and carries all trains into and out of Grand Central Terminal.

A state-of-good-repair effort to replace this critical bridge carrying Metro-North service into Grand Central Terminal. The project will ensure the safety of the public and railroad, improve rail reliability, build more resilient infrastructure, and reduce noise and vibration levels.

Critical state-of-good repair effort to replace the over 100-year-old Park Avenue Viaduct in Manhattan. Pre-construction work includes surveying, soil boring, rock core sampling, and geoprobes. Substructure construction (new foundations and columns) starting later this summer. Will include an MTA Arts & Design art installation over E 116 Street.

Phase 2 of the Park Avenue Viaduct replacement on Metro-North Railroad, from East 127th Street to midblock between East 131st and East 132nd Streets. Performed under contract MN-171020 with Halmar International, LLC.
Large-scale replacement project for the Park Avenue Viaduct which dates back to the late 1800s. Phase 1 spans from 115 to 123 Streets.

MTA Construction & Development is currently in the midst of a large-scale replacement project for the Park Avenue Viaduct. Phase 1 spans from 115th to 123rd Streets on Park Avenue. Progress includes replacing 2,200 feet of steel third rail with new aluminum third rail, relocating signal power feeder and communication cables. One of two gantry systems has been erected. The structure dates back to the late 1800s and carries approximately 750 trains and thousands of customers to and from Grand Central Terminal every weekday.
